articles.title: OpenAI pulls the plug on Sora, the viral AI video app that sparked deepfake concerns
articles.summary: OpenAI said Tuesday that it was "saying goodbye to the Sora app" and that it would share more soon about how to preserve what users already created on the app.

articles.title: Rubio plans travel to France to sell Iran war to skeptical G7 allies
articles.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio will travel to France this week to try to sell America's skeptical Group of Seven allies on the Iran war that has sent global fuel prices soaring.

articles.title: LaGuardia Crash Tests New Port Authority Leader
articles.summary: Kathryn Garcia, who began leading the agency in February, consoled two injured firefighters and dealt with delays at the three big airports serving New York City.

articles.title: Iran Says ‘Non-Hostile’ Ships Can Sail Through the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Ships with no ties to Israel or the United States would be allowed to pass, the government said, but it was unclear if any vessels would try.

articles.title: Democrat Emily Gregory Wins Florida Special Election in Mar-a-Lago’s District
articles.summary: Emily Gregory’s victory in Palm Beach brought the Democratic surge to President Trump’s backyard, while a union leader leads in a race for a state senate seat vacated by Florida’s lieutenant governor.

articles.title: Air Canada Pilots Killed in LaGuardia Crash Were Early in Flying Careers
articles.summary: The pilots were identified as Antoine Forest, 30, and Mackenzie Gunther. They were the only two fatalities in the plane’s collision with a fire truck at New York’s LaGuardia Airport.

articles.title: N.C. Senator Phil Berger Officially Just Lost to Sam Page
articles.summary: Phil Berger had money, power and an endorsement from President Trump. But his critics had a long list of resentments and, on Tuesday, he lost by a mere 23 votes.

articles.title: Judge says government's Anthropic ban looks like punishment
articles.summary: Tech company Anthropic, the maker of the Claude AI system, is suing the Trump administration over the government labeling it a "supply chain risk."

articles.title: An air traffic controller was juggling extra roles during the LaGuardia plane crash
articles.summary: The National Transportation Safety Board said it has concerns about air traffic controllers who work the midnight shift taking on extra work in an airspace as busy as LaGuardia's.

articles.title: Senate Republicans Again Block Bid to Halt Iran War Without Authorization
articles.summary: Senate Democrats failed for the third time to advance a resolution that would force the removal of troops from hostilities in Iran unless Congress approves offensive operations.

articles.title: New Mexico jury says Meta harms children's mental health and safety, violating state law
articles.summary: The jury agreed that Meta engaged in "unconscionable" trade practices that unfairly took advantage of the vulnerabilities of and inexperience of children. Jurors found there were thousands of violatio

articles.title: Newsom Says He Regrets Remarks Comparing Israel to ‘Apartheid State’
articles.summary: The California governor suggested that he had meant to refer to Israel’s potential future direction, not its current policies. “I revere the state of Israel,” he said.

articles.title: United States Said to Have Sent Iran a Plan to End the Middle East War
articles.summary: The 15-point plan was delivered via Pakistan, whose army chief has emerged as the key interlocutor between the United States and Iran, officials say.

articles.title: Cuba sends doctors on medical missions. The U.S. isn't a fan
articles.summary: It's a major source of revenue for the island. And it's controversial. Now countries are sending Cuban doctors home in response to pressure from the Trump administration.

articles.title: Senate confirms Trump's pick for new role of fraud enforcement at Justice Department
articles.summary: The confirmation comes just days after the White House announced details of its own task force to pursue fraud in government programs.

articles.title: The Israeli military wants several more weeks to fight Iran war, officials say
articles.summary: The Israeli military estimates it would need several more weeks of fighting to complete its war goals in Iran, at a time when President Trump says the U.S. is negotiating an end to the war.

articles.title: Will President Trump act on his threat to take Cuba?
articles.summary: New Yorker writer Jon Lee Anderson describes conditions in Cuba, why it's vulnerable now — and what regime change would mean — considering the Castro family's entrenchment in the Cuban government.

articles.title: Senate confirms Sen. Mullin as DHS secretary. And, Iran denies U.S. talks to end war
articles.summary: The Senate has confirmed Markwayne Mullin as the next Department of Homeland Security secretary. And, Iran has denied that it's in talks with the U.S. to end the war, which is now in its fourth week.

articles.title: Trump declares victory and claims Iran offers a 'prize' in talks Iran has denied having
articles.summary: President Trump said on Tuesday Iran made a valuable offer to reach a deal with the United States, in talks that Iran has denied are taking place. "We've won this. This war has been won," Trump said.

articles.title: Kim vows to 'irreversibly' cement North Korea's nuclear status
articles.summary: In his speech, Kim expressed pride in the country's rapid expansion of nuclear weapons and missiles in recent years, calling it the "right" choice.

articles.title: Asia boosts coal use as Iran war squeezes global LNG supplies
articles.summary: Analysts say coal may stabilize supplies for now but they warn that continued reliance on the polluting fuel will worsen air pollution.

articles.title: Airstrikes may have destroyed Iran's last F-14s, ending a long, strange saga
articles.summary: The F-14 was made famous in Top Gun. The U.S. sold the planes to Iran in the 1970s, only for the two countries to become enemies. Iran kept its F-14s flying for decades in the face of U.S. sanctions.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
